title: Is air conditioning essential for coping with heatwaves? France might be changing its mind on the issue
subhead: While more and more people are installing air conditioning, this technology is still seen by many as a misguided response to climate change. Some experts have challenged that argument as France faces unusually high temperatures.

title: Lebanon ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah remains fragile under US-Iran pressure
German government stokes up the memory wars surrounding the 'displaced persons' of 1945
Thomas Piketty: 'To clarify its own stance and unite its ranks, the left must propose a referendum to adopt a national solidarity tax'
subhead: As the 2027 presidential campaign draws nearer in France, left-wing parties should take inspiration from a 1945 bill and propose a referendum on imposing a more stringent tax on the wealthy, writes economist Thomas Piketty. He believes up to €800 billion could be raised.

title: With SpaceX, Wall Street demonstrates its unmatched capacity to fund innovation
subhead: According to Goldman Sachs, American companies are expected to raise $225 billion in 2026. This record level is fueling investment by these firms, which has surged by 12% since the end of 2022.

title: Fête de la Musique: Our picks for concerts not to miss
subhead: Le Monde's Culture desk offers its selection of concerts taking place on Sunday, June 21, as part of the national Fête de la Musique, which has become a global phenomenon.

title: Does the decline in reading and the rise of screens signal the end of the democratic era?
subhead: While concerns about the weakening of the mind in the face of new technologies are nothing new, the digital revolution and artificial intelligence now point to a possible end of book civilization.

'They call us misfits, degenerates': Free parties fall under French government scrutiny
subhead: Thousands of people gathered on June 6 in the south of France for a rave party, despite the events being banned locally until the end of the year. Participants defend them as among the last spaces of freedom outside of commercial and institutional frameworks.

title: In Trump's polarized America, empathy faces unanimous opposition
subhead: Once valued in American public life, empathy now faces criticism from the far right, which sees it as a sign of weakness. The left, too, is questioning its relevance: In a world where brutality prevails, can we still afford to show understanding?

title: Wealthy taxpayers clean out their holdings, keeping their homes, sports cars and gold bars safe
subhead: The new 20% tax on 'luxury' assets held in personal holdings is prompting owners to quickly remove them. As a result, the measure risks bringing in almost nothing.
